# Part II — Geo-Strategy prediction evaluation

**Predictive History, Volume I — Geo-Strategy**  
**As of:** 2026-03-23 (UTC)  
**Source registry:** [`prediction-tracking/registry/predictions.jsonl`](../prediction-tracking/registry/predictions.jsonl)

This Part II consolidates **prediction rows** registered for the Geo-Strategy lecture series (**Volume I Part I is twenty chapters / lectures** in `metadata/book-architecture.yaml`). The **narrative sections and table below** were drafted for the **original twelve-lecture** spine; **jiang-GS13-001 … jiang-GS20-003** now live in the same registry and should be folded into the next full news pass. It uses **open-web reporting** (news wires, specialist trackers, reference articles) to triangulate **dated** and **conditional** claims; **trend** and **interpretive** claims are scored only where metrics exist, otherwise marked **not evaluable** or **pending**.

---

## 1. Method (how to read the verdicts)

- **Supported / contradicted** — reserved mainly for **event** claims with clear outcomes in public record (elections, nominations, election dates).
- **Pending** — evaluation window not ended, or **antecedent** of a conditional not satisfied, or **definition** of “war,” “major hot war,” “invasion,” etc. still disputed.
- **Not evaluable** — no operational metric in the registry (e.g. “dominant ideology for 50 years,” global population 8B→1B without mechanism).
- **Triangulation** — where stakes are high (e.g. Iran escalation), this Part II cites **multiple independent types** of source (e.g. encyclopedic summary + conflict monitor + wire). One outlet echoing another is weak confirmation.

**Important:** The lecture series sometimes encodes **tension** between rows (e.g. US “wins” a hypothetical Iran war vs US “loses” and order changes). Part II **preserves** that tension; it does not force a single score for “the series.”

---

---

## 3. June 2025 — Iran escalation (cross‑cutting evidence)

Multiple independent summaries document **June 2025** as a **breakthrough escalation**: **Israel–Iran** exchange, then **United States** strikes on **Iranian nuclear-related sites** (often described as **Operation Midnight Hammer** in secondary coverage). See:

- Wikipedia summary: [June 2025 United States strikes against Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/June_2025_United_States_strikes_against_Iran)  
- **ISW** (Institute for the Study of War) **Iran Update** materials for **June 2025** (e.g. [ISW](https://www.understandingwar.org/) backgrounder series).  
- General-interest coverage (e.g. [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2025/06/15/nx-s1-5434172/israel-iran-strikes)) on **Israel–Iran** strikes.

**Why this matters for Part II:** Many rows ask about **“war,”” US–Iran war,”** or **“invasion.”** A **strike campaign** and **ceasefire** (as reported in those sources) **support** a claim of **major US military use of force against Iran** in a way **2024** did not. They **do not** automatically satisfy **“ground invasion”** (GS01-001) or **regime-change** scenarios. **Definitions** are doing the work.

## 5. Overall evaluation (Geo-Strategy corpus → forecasts)

**What scored cleanly (electoral facts)**  
The series correctly anticipated **Trump’s 2024 general-election win** in multiple redundant rows (**GS03-001, GS05-001**). That is **high-confidence** in public record.

**What scored clearly wrong**  
- **VP pick:** **Haley** prediction (**GS05-002**) **failed** against the **Vance** nomination.  
- **2024 turnout:** “**Lowest**” superlative (**GS11-004**) **failed** against **high-turnout** data ([Pew](https://www.pewresearch.org/politics/2025/06/26/voter-turnout-2020-2024/), [Ballotpedia](https://ballotpedia.org/Election_results,_2024:_Analysis_of_voter_turnout_in_the_2024_general_election), etc.).  
- **Iran election branch:** **Mokhber** presidency (**GS07-001**) **failed** against **Pezeshkian**.

**Where the record is strongest but not “true/false”**  
The **Iran** cluster is **directionally** consistent with **major escalation** in **2025** (Israel–Iran conflict, **US** strikes on **Iran**), which **supports** readings that treat **US–Iran military confrontation** as a **live** scenario rather than a **remote** one. Whether that **matches** each row’s wording (“invasion,” “major hot war,” “lose war”) is **not** a single button.

**Internal tension**  
The registry **flags** tension between **US wins** if invades (**GS01-003**) and **US loses** / order shifts (**GS03-003**). Part II **does not** resolve that; it is a **feature** of the lecture arc for readers to track.

**Net**  
Geo-Strategy’s **dated US electoral** calls were **strong** where duplicated. **Specific** bets (**VP, turnout superlative, Mokhber**) **missed**. The **Middle East** cluster **aged** into **real** **2025** **fighting** and **US** **strikes**, but **philosophy** and **scenario** language still **require** **definition** before a **single** **“accuracy score”** is **meaningful**.

---

## 6. Maintenance

- **Registry updates** — When a row is **resolved**, update `predictions.jsonl` and run `python3 scripts/work_jiang/rebuild_registry_db.py` per project workflow.  
- **This document** — Revise **as-of** date when re-running news passes.  
- **Stronger citation** — For book publication, extend `excerpt` / add **span** links into `lectures/*.md` transcripts.

---

*Part II consolidates all 38 registry predictions for Predictive History Vol. I — Geo-Strategy. It is not a substitute for the original lectures or transcripts.*
